title: Pyrus takhtajanii
taxonomy: Pyrus takhtajanii is classified within the family Rosaceae, which includes other fruit-bearing trees such as apples and pears. This classification places Pyrus takhtajanii in the order Rosales. At the family level, Pyrus takhtajanii is often grouped alongside other deciduous trees like Prunus and Sorbus. The genus Pyrus is a group of pear species, with Pyrus takhtajanii being a specific species within this group. Pyrus takhtajanii is also classified as a flowering plant, specifically a woody perennial.

preparations: Pyrus takhtajanii is a species of pyrus native to Iran and was named after Michail Georgievich Takhtajan. It is a shrub or small tree with fragrant flowers and edible fruit. intro: Pyrus takhtajanii, also known as Takhtajan's pear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus takhtajanii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pyrus takhtajanii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
